# attestto-desktop

> Open-source Electron app that turns every computer into a node in a national identity mesh. Citizens, lawyers, notaries, and government officials use it to verify identity, sign documents, manage credentials, and contribute storage to the distributed infrastructure.

Part of the [Attestto Open](https://attestto.org) ecosystem.

**27 tests passing. 80% coverage. Apache 2.0.**

## What it is

The **"Station"** — where the heavy lifting happens: biometric liveness verification, multi-page notarial document signing, social recovery key management, and mesh node operation. The mobile PWA (`attestto-app`) is the everyday wallet; the desktop app is the full identity workstation.

Every installation contributes 250 MB (configurable) to the P2P identity mesh, hosting encrypted data it cannot read — the Blind Courier principle. In return, the user's own identity state is replicated across 50+ peers.

## Core systems

### Encrypted vault
- OS keychain (`safeStorage`) + biometric (Touch ID / Windows Hello) — no passwords
- `xsalsa20-poly1305` encryption with scrypt KDF
- Algorithm-agile envelope (`cryptoSuite` field) — post-quantum migration path without breaking existing vaults
- 15-minute auto-lock with biometric re-authentication
- Holds: identity keys, verifiable credentials, biometric proofs, settings, persona

### Identity verification (plugin architecture)
- **Biometric liveness** (built-in) — TensorFlow.js face mesh, 100% local, anti-spoofing
  - Voice-guided challenge: center → left → right → up → blink
  - Web Speech API narration
  - Biometric captures double-encrypted in vault; hash-based audit trail
- **Document verification** — country plugin slot (each country implements its ID types: cédula, DNI, passport)
- **KYC connectors** — Sumsub, Onfido, Jumio integration slots
- All verification paths produce a Verifiable Credential stored in the local vault

### Social recovery (Shamir 2-of-3)
- Vault encrypted with passkey-derived key → split into 3 shards via Shamir Secret Sharing
- Shards distributed to 3 guardians via the P2P mesh
- Recovery: 2 shards + biometric → full vault reconstruction on new device
- Guardians hold opaque encrypted blobs — two colluding guardians cannot reconstruct keys without the device

### P2P mesh node
- Runs `@attestto/mesh` (libp2p + Kademlia DHT + GossipSub)
- Auto country detection via system timezone → joins correct national mesh
- Gossip-based revocation — tombstones propagate in <500ms
- Offline verification — verify credentials and signed documents without internet

### Module system
- Universal core + country-specific modules from independent registries
- Core: `@attestto/proctor` (exam proctoring), `@attestto/sign` (document signing), `@attestto/verify` (verification)
- Country modules: each country runs its own registry (`registry.attestto.cr/modules.json`)
- Third-party modules: hospitals, banks, universities publish to their own registries
- Security sandbox: modules get scoped API surface, no raw key access

## Architecture

```
Electron Main Process
  MeshNode (libp2p)     — P2P networking
  MeshStore (SQLite)    — local encrypted blob storage
  Vault (xsalsa20)      — key and credential storage

Electron Renderer Process
  Vue 3 + Quasar + Pinia — UI
  TensorFlow.js          — biometric liveness (local)
  Module Registry        — dynamic tab loading

IPC Bridge (preload.ts)
  Exposes vault, mesh, signing APIs to renderer
  Blocks dangerous APIs — renderer has no Node access
```

## Stack

- Electron (main + renderer + preload)
- Vue 3 + Quasar + TypeScript
- Pinia (state management)
- electron-vite (build tooling)
- `@attestto/mesh` — P2P node
- TensorFlow.js (`@tensorflow-models/face-landmarks-detection`) — biometric liveness
- `@noble/curves` + `tweetnacl` — Ed25519 + xsalsa20-poly1305
- `electron-updater` — auto-updates via GitHub Releases

## Repo layout

```
src/
  main/          — Electron main process (mesh node, vault, IPC handlers)
  renderer/      — Vue 3 app (UI, identity verification, document signing)
  preload/       — IPC bridge (contextBridge — no raw Node in renderer)
  shared/        — types shared across processes
resources/       — icons, installer assets
docs/            — architecture docs, module authoring guide
tests/           — 27 tests (Vitest + Electron test utils)
electron-builder.yml — packaging config (Mac, Windows, Linux)
electron.vite.config.ts
```

## Development

```bash
pnpm install
pnpm dev      # start Electron in development mode
pnpm test     # run 27 tests
pnpm build    # package for current platform
```

## Distribution

Packaged via `electron-builder`. Auto-updates via GitHub Releases (`electron-updater`). Supports Mac (dmg/zip), Windows (nsis), Linux (AppImage/deb).

## Country module authoring

Modules are independent packages published to a country registry. They receive a sandboxed API:

```typescript
import type { DesktopModule } from '@attestto/module-sdk'

const myCountryModule: DesktopModule = {
  id: 'cr-notary',
  name: 'CR Notary Dashboard',
  requiredCredentials: ['BarAssociationMembership'],
  tabs: [{ label: 'Sign Deed', component: SignDeedTab }],
  onInstall: async (api) => {
    // api.vault — read/write credentials (scoped)
    // api.mesh  — publish/retrieve encrypted data
    // api.sign  — request document signing
    // api.verify — verify a credential
  },
}
```

## Related repos

| Repo | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `attestto-mesh` | P2P data layer running inside this app |
| `attestto-mobile` | Camera companion PWA — phone captures documents for desktop |
| `attestto-app` | Mobile-first PWA wallet (everyday use) |
| `attestto-verify` | `@attestto/verify` web component used for in-app verification |
| `attestto-trust` | PKI trust roots used for document signature validation |
